items.title: What's New notifications
items.description: A bell icon in the header now shows a badge when there's something new — click it to see what's changed.

items.title: Install Ascent as an app
items.description: Ascent can now be installed on your phone or desktop and works offline once installed.

items.title: Better contrast in dark theme
items.description: Priority badges and filter chips are easier to read in dark theme.

items.title: Share a read-only link to your roadmap
items.description: Publish a snapshot of your roadmap and share the link with anyone — they can view your progress without signing in, and your notes are never included.

items.title: Export your roadmap to your calendar
items.description: Export to a calendar file (.ics) so your Daily Todos show up alongside the rest of your schedule.

items.title: Print or save your roadmap as a PDF
items.description: Print your roadmap or save it as a branded PDF, with your progress and priorities laid out clearly on every page.

items.title: Review reminders for topics you've completed
items.description: Completed topics resurface for review after two weeks — a new "Review" filter shows what's due, and you can mark a topic reviewed to reset its clock.

items.title: Group review reminders by tag
items.description: Add tags to a topic from its edit panel, and topics due for review get grouped by shared tag so related material comes back to you together.

items.title: Favorite your most-used roadmaps
items.description: Star up to three roadmaps to pin them to the top of your roadmap picker.

items.title: Streak freeze
items.description: Miss a single day and your streak is automatically protected once every 7 days — no action needed.

items.title: Track time spent per topic
items.description: Start and pause a timer on any topic to track how long you've spent on it, with a running total on your Progress page.

items.title: Local reminders for Daily Todos
items.description: Turn on reminders to get a notification 15 minutes before a Daily Todo is due.

items.title: Celebrate finishing a phase or roadmap
items.description: Complete every topic in a phase or roadmap and you'll get a short celebration — a fun way to mark the milestone.

items.title: A new look — Alpenglow
items.description: Ascent has a new visual design across every page: warm neutrals, a refreshed gold-and-rose accent, and clearer cards and checklists.

items.title: Check whether a resource link still works
items.description: Every resource in a topic's edit panel now has a "Check link" button that reports whether it looks reachable. This only runs when you click it, and it can't always tell for sure — some sites can't be checked from your browser.

items.title: Search topics across all of your roadmaps
items.description: The command palette (Cmd/Ctrl+K) now searches topic titles, notes, and resource links across every roadmap you've started, not just the one you're viewing.

items.title: Compare your roadmap against its template or another roadmap
items.description: A new "Compare roadmaps" button on the Progress page shows which topics you've added, which are missing, and which are done.

items.title: Weekly progress digest
items.description: The dashboard now shows a dismissible summary of last week's activity, like how many topics you completed and your current streak.

items.title: Duplicate-topic warnings when importing a roadmap
items.description: "Create your own roadmap" now flags topics that look like duplicates across phases after you paste in a roadmap, so you can review and merge them.

items.title: "Create your own roadmap" now saves your draft
items.description: Your topic list and customization choices are saved automatically while the "Create your own roadmap" modal is open, so closing it by accident no longer loses your work.

items.title: Better detection of unfilled or incomplete AI responses
items.description: "Create your own roadmap" now catches AI responses that are placeholder text or a refusal instead of a real roadmap, and tells you clearly instead of importing them as-is.

items.title: Feature tour now covers everything in the app
items.description: The first-time walkthrough now includes Settings, roadmap sharing, backups, feedback, and the "What's New" bell. A second short tour introduces Daily Todos, favorite roadmaps, and "Create your own roadmap" the first time you visit "My Roadmaps."

items.title: Pasting a very large roadmap no longer freezes the tab
items.description: "Create your own roadmap" now checks the size of a pasted roadmap before trying to read it, and shows a clear message if you pasted something too large, like a whole conversation instead of just the roadmap.

items.title: A limit on how many custom roadmaps you can create
items.description: You can now have up to 25 custom or AI-imported roadmaps. If you hit the limit, Ascent explains it and suggests deleting an older or unused roadmap first.

items.title: Button text is now centered
items.description: Labels on every button are now centered instead of aligned to the left.

items.title: Clearer AI-import prompt, fewer round-trips
items.description: The generated prompt in "Create your own roadmap" now spells out the exact rules (length limits, what not to paste) so an AI is more likely to get it right on the first try.
